zh.Budenheim, Mainz-Bingen, Rheinland-Pfalz: 德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 麥音-炳布登海姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Otto II. Graf von Habsburg於1057年出生在瑞士阿高哈布斯堡時，他的父親Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg27歲，母親Regulinde von Nellenburg30歲。他於大約1091年與Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette在瑞士阿高哈布斯堡結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1112年11月8日在德國海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 麥音-炳布登海姆享年55歲，埋葬在瑞士阿高姆利。

zh-hans.Budenheim, Mainz-Bingen, Rheinland-Pfalz: 德国海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 麥音-炳布登海姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Otto II. Graf von Habsburg于1057年出生在瑞士阿高哈布斯堡时，他的父亲Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg27岁，母亲Regulinde von Nellenburg30岁。他于大约1091年与Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette在瑞士阿高哈布斯堡结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1112年11月8日在德国海藍-普法爾茲藍德可萊斯 麥音-炳布登海姆享年55岁，埋葬在瑞士阿高姆利。

nameMeanings.name: Graf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Hermann, Horst, Manfred, Udo, Alois.

German (also Gräf): status name from Middle High German grāve, grābe, which was used as a title denoting various more or less aristocratic dignitaries and officials. In later times it became established as a title of nobility equivalent to the Romance count. The vocabulary word also denoted a variety of different minor local functionaries in different parts of Germany. In the Grand Duchy of Hesse, for example, it was used for the holder of the comparatively humble office of village headman (compare Mayer , Schulz , and Vogt ). The surname could have originated from any of these senses or be a metonymic occupational or status name for a servant or retainer of a count, or a nickname for someone who gave himself airs and graces. This surname is also found in many other European countries, for example in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Hungary, Poland, Czechia, Slovakia, Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of the corresponding Hungarian and Slavic surnames Gróf and Grof . Compare Graef .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name selected, like   Herzog   and other words denoting titles, because of their aristocratic connotations.

variants.name: Grebe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Erwin, Fritz, Hans, Willi.
German (Hesse): variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: De Graaf
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Adriaan, Bastiaan, Cornelis, Dirk, Geert.

Dutch: from graaf ‘count’ (see Graf ), with the addition of the definite article de ‘the’, hence a nickname for the servant of a count or for someone supposedly resembling one, and also a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named with this word.

variants.name: Graefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Armin, Arno, Goetz, Horst, Johannes.
German (North Rhine-Westphalia; also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Grefe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erhardt, Gerhard, Gerhart, Kurt.
North German: variant of   Graf  .

variants.name: Greve
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Otto, Reinhold, Bernhardt, Claus, Erna, Erwin, Ewald, Fritz, Kurt.
North German and Scandinavian: status name from Middle Low German and Danish greve ‘count’, equivalent to German Graf .
Dutch: vowel variant of Grave ‘count’, or a topographic name from Middle Dutch greve ‘ditch, moat, channel’.

variants.name: Croff
meanings: Altered form of German   Graf   or   Graff  . Or of Dutch   Kroft  .

variants.name: Mayer
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Franz, Helmut, Wilhelm, Erwin, Gerhard, Heinz, Ilse, Lorenz, Wolfgang.

English (Staffordshire): post-medieval form of Mear, which is either a topographic name, from Middle English mere ‘pond, pool’ (Old English mere) or Middle English mere ‘boundary’ (Old English (ge)mǣre), or a habitational name from one or more of the many places with names derived from these terms, such as Mere (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Wiltshire), Meir in Caverswall (Staffordshire), and Meare (Somerset).
English: occupational name for a physician, from Old French mire, Middle English mire, meir, meyre, originally synonymous with Myer .

variants.name: Grafe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Fritz, Ralf.
German (also Gräfe): variant of Graf 1.

variants.name: Graef
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Egon, Lothar, Otto, Armin, Claus, Erwin, Franz, Friedrich, Hans, Helmut, Hermann, Jochen.
German (also Gräf): see Graf .
Dutch: variant of Graaf, see De Graaf .

variants.name: Graff
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) (also Gräff): variant of Graf . Compare Graeff .

English (London): metonymic occupational name for a clerk or scribe, from Old French grafe ‘stylus, pencil’, a derivative of Old French grafer ‘to write’ (from Late Latin grafare, from Greek graphein). It is uncertain whether the English name survived the medieval period or whether the early 17th century bearers in Britain were in fact of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Otto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a short form of any of the various Germanic compound personal names containing the element od, ot ‘prosperity, wealth’ ( compare the corresponding Old English ēad in names such as Edward and Edwin ). St Otto of Bamberg ( d. 1139 ) was a missionary to the Pomeranians. Otto the Great ( 912–73 ) is generally regarded as the founder of the Holy Roman Empire, and the name has been borne by several members of German and Austrian royal houses. It was recorded occasionally among immigrants to England from the Low Countries in the 14th century, but failed to establish itself. Since the 19th century it has again been used occasionally in the English-speaking world, mostly among immigrants from Germanic countries.

nameMeanings.name: II.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Otto II. Graf von Habsburg was born in 1057, in Habsburg, Aargau, Switzerland, his father, Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg, was 27 and his mother, Regulinde von Nellenburg, was 30. He married Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette about 1091, in Habsburg, Aargau, Switzerland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. He died on 8 November 1112, in Budenheim, Mainz-Bingen, Rhineland-Palatinate, Germany, at the age of 55, and was buried in Muri, Aargau, Switzerland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Otto II. Graf von Habsburg est né en 1057, à Habsburg, Argovie, Suisse, son père, Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Regulinde von Nellenburg, avait 30 ans. Il a épousé Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette environ 1091, à Habsburg, Argovie, Suisse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 8 novembre 1112, à Budenheim, Landkreis Mainz-Bingen, Rheinland-Pfalz, Allemagne, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Muri, Argovie, Suisse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Otto II. Graf von Habsburg 1057 in Habsburg, Aargau, Schweiz geboren wurde, war sein Vater Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg 27 und seine Mutter Regulinde von Nellenburg 30. Er hat um 1091 in Habsburg, Aargau, Schweiz Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 8. November 1112 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Budenheim, Landkreis Mainz-Bingen, Rheinland-Pfalz, Deutschland und wurde in Muri, Aargau, Schweiz begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Otto II. Graf von Habsburg è nato il nel 1057 nel Habsburg, Argovia, Svizzera, il suo padre, Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg, aveva 27 anni e il suo madre, Regulinde von Nellenburg, aveva 30 anni. Lui sposato Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette circa 1091, nel Habsburg, Argovia, Svizzera. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì il 8 novembre 1112, nel Budenheim, Landkreis Mainz-Bingen, Rheinland-Pfalz, Germania, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Muri, Argovia, Svizzera.

ja.lifeSummary: Otto II. Graf von Habsburgが1057年にスイスアールガウハブスブルグで生まれた時，父Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburgは27歳，母Regulinde von Nellenburgは30歳であった。約1091年にスイスアールガウハブスブルグでHilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferretteと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1112年11月8日にドイツルヘインランド-プファルズランドクレイス メインズ-ビンジェンブデンヘイムで55歳で死去し，スイスアールガウムリにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Otto II. Graf von Habsburg(이)가 1057년 스위스 아아르가 하브스버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg(은)는 27세였고, 그의 어머니 Regulinde von Nellenburg(은)는 30세였다. 그는 대략 1091년 스위스 아아르가 하브스버그에서 Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1112년 11월 8일 독일 리인랜드-p팔즈 랜드크레이스 맹즈-빙 부데하임에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 스위스 아아르가 머이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Otto II. Graf von Habsburg nasceu em 1057, em Habsburg, Aargau, Suiça, seu pai, Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Regulinde von Nellenburg, tinha 30 anos. Ele casou-se com Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette aproximadamente 1091, em Habsburg, Aargau, Suiça. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 8 de novembro de 1112, em Budenheim, Landkreis Mainz-Bingen, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemanha, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Muri, Aargau, Suiça.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Otto II. Graf von Habsburg родился в 1057 г. в местности хабсбург, ааргау, Швейцария, его отцу по имени Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Regulinde von Nellenburg, 30. Он женился на женщине по имени Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette около 1091 г. в местности хабсбург, ааргау, Швейцария. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Он умер 8 ноября 1112 г. в местности буденим, ландкрис меинз-бинген, раинланд-пфалз, Германия в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности мури, ааргау, Швейцария.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Otto II. Graf von Habsburg nació en 1057, en Habsburg, Aargau, Suiza, su papá, Werner II., Werner I. als Graf von Habsburg, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Regulinde von Nellenburg, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Hilda bzw. Ita von Pfirt bzw. Ferrette aproximadamente 1091, en Habsburg, Aargau, Suiza. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 1 hija. Murió el 8 de noviembre de 1112, en Budenheim, Landkreis Mainz-Bingen, Rheinland-Pfalz, Alemania, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Muri, Aargau, Suiza.
